title: Add --resume and --session-id for checkpoint/recovery of failed agents
source: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/sdk - Claude Code session management with --resume and --session-id flags
description: When an agent times out or crashes (delegate.py line 722), all progress is lost and the ticket is marked blocked. Claude Code supports --session-id (custom session tracking) and --resume SESSION_ID (continue from where it left off). By assigning deterministic session IDs based on ticket+agent and implementing retry-with-resume, the system can recover from transient failures instead of losing all agent work. This is especially valuable for long-running XL tickets and team sprints.
proposed_changes: 1. In delegate_to_agent(), generate a deterministic session ID from ticket_id + agent_role + attempt_number: session_id = f'cto-{ticket_id}-{agent_role}-{attempt}'. Add '--session-id', session_id to the cmd list. 2. Store session_id in the ticket JSON under a new 'agent_sessions' field: {agent_role: {session_id: str, started_at: str, status: str}}. 3. Add retry logic: on TimeoutExpired or non-zero exit, check if attempt < max_retries (default 2), then re-invoke with '--resume', session_id and '--continue' flags to pick up where the agent left off. 4. In orchestrate.py run_delegate(), pass session tracking metadata through to delegate.py. 5. Update the sprint loop to check for resumable sessions before starting fresh — if a ticket has an active session_id from a previous failed attempt, resume it instead of starting over. 6. Add --no-retry flag to delegate CLI for cases where retry is not desired (e.g., dry-run mode).
